svg.text: Typical Firecrawl CLI execution flow
1) Parse command + normalize input
URL shorthand (firecrawl https://...), option parsing, command dispatch (Commander)
2) Resolve auth/config
Config precedence: CLI flags > env vars > stored credentials; preAction hook enforces auth for core commands
Exception: custom --api-url skips mandatory cloud auth (useful for self-hosted/local Firecrawl)
3A) Synchronous path
scrape / map / search
Single request-response, optional multi-URL concurrency
download = map first, then batched scrape + nested file write
3B) Job-based async path
crawl / agent / browser sessions
Start job or session, optionally --wait with polling + spinner/progress
Job IDs become stable handles for status checks and resumable workflows
4) Output shaping
Raw text for single text formats (markdown/html/links/images/summary); JSON for multi-format or complex payloads
Writes to stdout by default; -o writes files (auto-creates directories). .firecrawl/ used as local cache/workspace
5) Integrate into builder workflow
Shell pipes, CI jobs, AI coding agents via setup skills/MCP, and experimental multi-agent workflows (claude/codex/opencode)
Operational guardrails: credit visibility, concurrency limits, retries/timeouts, controllable scraping depth
